test_cases.purpose: Sample test 1 from problem statement. Verifies basic partitioning into 2 groups with a non-trivial split.
Sample test 2: N = K forces each element into its own group. Answer is max element.
Sample test 3: moderate case with 3 groups. Optimal split [6,1],[2,4],[3,2] gives max 7.
All elements equal. Optimal split [3,3],[3,3] gives max 6. Tests uniform input handling.
Minimum possible input: N=1, K=1. Single experiment must go to single lab.
K = N with varying elements. Each lab gets one experiment, answer is max element = 5.
K=2 with a dominant element in the middle. The large element 100 must be grouped with neighbors. Best split: [1,1,100] and [1,1] → max(102, 2) = 102.
All elements identical (7), K=3. Optimal: [7,7],[7,7],[7,7] → max = 14.
K=1 forces all elements into one group. Tests large value handling (answer exceeds 32-bit int range).
Breaks wrong approach #1 (sort and distribute). Sorting gives [1,1,1,10,10,10], round-robin yields max(12,21) = 21. Correct answer with contiguous groups: [10,1,1] and [10,1,1] → max = 12.
Breaks wrong approach #2 (equal-count partition). Equal-count gives [3,1,1]=5, [1,1,1]=3, [1,3]=4 → max=5. Optimal: [3,1]=4, [1,1,1,1]=4, [1,3]=4 → max=4.

Breaks wrong approach #3 (ceil(sum/K)). sum=28, ceil(28/3)=10, but 10 is infeasible. With max=10: [1,2,3,4]=10, [5]=5, [6,3]=9, [4]=4 needs 4 labs. Optimal: max=11 gives [1,2,3,4]=10, [5,6]=11, [3,4]=7 in 3 labs.
Small N with all elements at max value 10⁹ and K=2. Optimal: [10⁹,10⁹],[10⁹,10⁹] → max = 2×10⁹. Tests 64-bit integer handling (answer exceeds 32-bit int range) with a real executable input.
All elements at max value 10⁹, K=3. Optimal: [10⁹,10⁹],[10⁹,10⁹],[10⁹] → max = 2×10⁹. Tests overflow boundary (answer exceeds 32-bit int).
All 10 elements at 10⁹, K=3. Optimal: [4×10⁹],[4×10⁹],[2×10⁹] → max = 4×10⁹. Tests large answer values.

stress_test_config.comparison: Run brute_force_solution (enumerate all C(N-1,K-1) partitions) and reference solution (binary search + greedy) on each random input. Compare outputs. Any mismatch indicates a bug. Generate random N in [1,50], random K in [1,N], random D[i] in [1,100].

edge_cases_covered: K=N each element in its own group
K=1 all elements in one group
Dominant element in middle (forces suboptimal grouping)
Boundary values with K=2 at max element values
wrong_approaches_tested: Wrong approach #1 (sort and distribute): tested by adv_1 — D=[10,1,1,10,1,1], K=2. Sorting gives max=21, correct answer is 12.
Wrong approach #2 (equal-count partition): tested by adv_2 — D=[3,1,1,1,1,1,1,3], K=3. Equal-count gives max=5, correct answer is 4.
Wrong approach #3 (ceil(sum/K)): tested by adv_3 — D=[1,2,3,4,5,6,3,4], K=3. ceil(28/3)=10, correct answer is 11.
